scores.one_line_justification: Clean, well-tested delivery (throttle retention, mode-switch stranding, audit reasons all pinned) but send_digests never consults in_quiet_hours, so a flush at 23:00 local delivers non-urgent content in violation of R5, and the interaction is neither tested nor recorded anywhere.

send_digests ignores quiet hours at flush time (R5 x R11 miss) and test_digest_mode_ignores_quiet_hours_at_queue_time actively pins the violating flush-at-23:00 behaviour, while models.py's Delivery docstring is left stale ('currently "immediate"') despite the new digest kind.

Thorough tests (queue-then-flush audit sequence, throttle keeps queue across days, unsubscribed gate before digest) and faithful R9/R11 spec text, but send_digests never checks quiet hours, so a flush during the user's 22:00-07:00 window delivers, and the interaction is untested and unrecorded.

Only delivery that honours quiet hours at flush time (checked before draining, tested, and recorded in R11), though digest queuing appends no queued audit entry (R9's one-entry-per-attempt invariant weakened, R9/R6 not extended) and a throttled flush drains and silently drops the whole batch rather than keeping it pending.

Strong tests (the only workspace pinning one throttle send for a multi-notification digest; throttled batch survives to next day) and the quiet-hours call is at least recorded in R11, but the flush still delivers during quiet hours while unamended R5 forbids non-urgent delivery then, and queued notifications leave no audit trace.
